channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump asks Supreme Court for permission to resume deporting Venezuelan migrants under wartime law
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday asked the Supreme Court for permission to resume deportations of Venezuelan migrants to El Salvador under an 18th century wartime law, while a court fight continues.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Snake covered by Endangered Species Act killed at Michigan park
item.description: Officials are urging “understanding” over “fear” after a federally protected snake was recently found killed at the Matthaei Botanical Gardens and Nichols Arboretum in Ann Arbor.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Law firm Jenner & Block sues over Trump executive order, becoming second firm to do so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prominent law firm targeted by President Donald Trump called a White House executive order “an unconstitutional abuse of power against lawyers, their clients, and the legal system” in a federal lawsuit filed Friday that urged a judge to block its enforcement.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Utah becomes the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water
item.description: Utah has become the first state to ban fluoride in public drinking water, despite widespread opposition from dentists and national health organizations.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Officers in Tyre Nichols case back in court ahead of upcoming trial
item.description: Three of the former Memphis Police Officers accused in the beating death of Tyre Nichols are expected in court on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Katie Copeland, NewsNation

item.title: Musk to travel to Wisconsin ahead of judicial race, hand out $1M checks
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk announced late Thursday he would be traveling to Wisconsin ahead of a high-stakes state Supreme Court race to talk with voters and hand out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: Lawyer for Michigan couple held in Mexican prison says resort company is at fault
item.description: A Michigan couple remains behind bars in a Mexican prison, accused of breaching a timeshare contract — but their lawyer said the resort that filed the criminal complaint against them breached the contract first.
item.dc:creator: Shajaka Shelton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Democrats outraged at Trump's changes pour millions of dollars into 2 special elections in Florida
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Democrats, with few electoral outlets for their outrage at President Donald Trump's dramatic restructuring of the federal government, are pouring millions of dollars into two special elections in Florida.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump restricts federal research funding, a lifeblood for colleges
item.description: After decades of partnership with the U.S. government, colleges are facing new doubts about the future of their federal funding.
item.dc:creator: SHARON LURYE and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump order targets 'divisive narratives' in Smithsonian museums
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday signed an executive order seeking to exert more control over what is displayed at Smithsonian museums and federal sites, citing what the White House called “divisive narratives” promoted by certain museums.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: A powerful new app for card collectors
item.description: To a card collector, there’s nothing quite like ripping open a fresh pack. “You find someone you like and you collect them… and I think it’s special,” started Jay Moslehi, also known as MOJO SPORTS on YouTube.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Musk promises to go to Wisconsin to personally deliver $2 million to voters in Supreme Court race
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk says he plans to hold a rally in Wisconsin to “personally hand over” $2 million to a pair of voters who have already cast their ballots in the state’s hotly contested Supreme Court race.
item.dc:creator: Scott Bauer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Sanctuary city officials rebuke police officers for assisting Border Patrol
item.description: Sanctuary city officials in upstate New York have sounded off on members of their own police force for coming to the aid of federal immigration agents who stopped a van carrying up to eight occupants.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Powerful earthquake rocks Thailand and Myanmar, kills more than 140
item.description: A 7.7 magnitude earthquake rocked Thailand and neighboring Myanmar midday on Friday, burying an unknown number when a high-rise building under construction collapsed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mahmoud Khalil's lawyers to appear in New Jersey court over jurisdiction of Columbia activist's case
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — Lawyers for Mahmoud Khalil, a Columbia University student the Trump administration is trying to expel from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Another federal judge blocks Trump policy banning transgender troops in the military
item.description: TACOMA, Wash. (AP) — A U.S. judge in Washington state has blocked enforcement of President Donald Trump’s order banning transgender people from serving in the military, the second nationwide injunction against the policy in as many weeks. The order Thursday from U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stanford, Cal and UCLA investigated in Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: The Trump administration says it has opened investigations into the admissions policies at Stanford University and three campuses within the University of California system, including UC Berkeley, UCLA and UC Irvine.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Gecker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Man accused of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas arrested, police say
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A man who set fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas and who painted the word “resist” for authorities to find at the scene has been arrested.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How this auto union leader's support for Trump's tariffs scrambled labor politics again
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has once again scrambled the politics of organized labor and the working class with his planned tariffs on auto imports.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yolanda Saldívar, killer of Selena, denied parole
item.description: The woman convicted of killing Tejano music legend Selena Quintanilla-Perez has been denied parole after spending decades behind bars for fatally shooting the young singer at a Texas motel in 1995, the state’s parole board announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: FAA acknowledges it must do better after deadly DC air collision
item.description: The head of the Federal Aviation Administration told Congress during a hearing Thursday about a midair collision over Washington, D.C., that killed 67 people that the agency must do more to ensure flying remains safe.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Caregiver was at nail salon when autistic woman went missing: Police
item.description: The caregiver initially told investigators DaLomba vanished while she was using the bathroom at the Burlington Coat Factory.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Bawden, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court refuses to halt an order for the rehiring of thousands of fired federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court in California has refused to halt a judge’s order requiring the Trump administration to rehire thousands of federal workers who were let go in mass firings. A split 9th U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: As many as 6 hurricanes forecast to strike US this season
item.description: “It only takes one landfall to create a devastating season,” AccuWeather chief meteorologist Jonathan Porter said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Rubio says at least 300 foreign students' visas have been revoked
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Thursday at least 300 foreign students have had their visas revoked amid the Trump administration's immigration agenda, a far higher number than was previously known.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Violin thief-turned-bank robber sentenced to 4 years in prison
item.description: A California man convicted of orchestrating a scheme to steal valuable violins, and then robbing a bank when he realized federal investigators were closing in on him, has been sentenced.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Illegal immigrant nabbed by Georgia police after allegedly preying on four children
item.description: An illegal immigrant from Guatemala was arrested in Georgia after allegedly molesting four children under the age of 14, according to the Valdosta Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DC plane crash victim's father says 'culture of complacency' allowed fatal accident to happen
item.description: The father of the American Airlines co-pilot who died in a midair collision near Reagan National Airport said a "culture of complacency" allowed the disaster to happen.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Young American tourists' cause of death revealed after they were found 'motionless' at beach resort
item.description: The cause of death for three Massachusetts women who were found dead in their Belize resort hotel room last month has been revealed by city officials.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Associated Press, banned from White House press pool, renews request to court for reinstatement
item.description: A lawyer for The Associated Press has asked a federal judge once again to reinstate the agency’s access to the White House press pool and some other official events.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DC-bound Amtrak passenger may have exposed travelers to measles, health officials say
item.description: The passenger rode an Amtrak train in the Northeast last week before visiting an urgent care center in DC, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Judge: High heat in Texas prisons unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge found the extreme heat in Texas prisons is “plainly unconstitutional” but did not order the state to begin installing air conditioners.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Appeals court halts Chutkan's order for DOGE records
item.description: A federal appeals court on Wednesday temporarily blocked an order from U.S. District Judge Tanya Chutkan directing Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to turn over records and answer questions about its efforts to slash the federal bureaucracy. A three-judge panel on the U.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Nurse Blake setting sail for NurseCon At Sea 2025
item.description: "We rent out the whole ship. We pack it with 3,000 nurses on board we are the safest cruise ship ever," Blake Lynch told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Pasadena reaches agreement to host Olympic soccer at Rose Bowl
item.description: The City of Pasadena has reached an agreement with the organizers of the 2028 Olympic and Paralympic Games to host Olympic soccer at Rose Bowl Stadium. Men's and women's semifinal and final matches will be held at the historic venue when Los Angeles hosts the games in 2028.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5
